text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet hamlet nestled among lush forests, there lived a woman named Amarantha. Amarantha lived alone atop a hill overlooking the valley below. She loved to gaze down upon a particular spot where a family of ducks seemed to reside. The ducks knew each other very well — there was Dooley, the wise old duck whose opinions mattered the most; Bella, the adventurous duckling; and the young scout duckling named Oliver.

One sunny afternoon, as Amarotha watched the ducks with a fond eye, she heard a soft voice. Doing a double-take, she saw a soft, golden-eyed kitten atop a fallen log. Naming the kitten Oliver seemed whimsical, but after several days, Oliver began to come out more often.

Over weeks, Oliver grew bold. One morning, Oliver climbed on top of a freshly harvested pear tree. Amarotha couldnkedooda that Oliver seemed eager to explore every nook.
